mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, driving positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction sector expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings rebound, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM changes yield negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI increases exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases combined with UNRATE decreases fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue fundamentals, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 QoQ growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines exceeding 1.0% month-over-month signal severe supply-side contraction and falling corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM < -1.0% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases.
